Machine Learning

An application-focused introduction to machine learning methods, model development, and critical evaluation for scientific datasets.

Audience: Bachelor's and master's students

Course Type: Teaching

Format: Conceptual lectures paired with practical modeling workflows

Supervised learning Unsupervised learning Regression Classification Clustering Model evaluation

Supervised learning workflows

Students build intuition for predictive modeling tasks and the assumptions behind common algorithms.

  • Regression for continuous targets
  • Classification for categorical outcomes
  • Training, validation, and generalization

Unsupervised learning and structure discovery

The course covers exploratory learning methods that reveal patterns without labeled targets.

  • Clustering and grouping structure
  • Distance metrics and representation choices
  • Interpreting latent patterns in data

Evaluation and interpretation

Strong emphasis is placed on understanding why a model performs the way it does and how to communicate results responsibly.

  • Validation strategies and performance metrics
  • Error analysis and model comparison
  • Practical interpretation of machine learning outputs

The course balances methodological foundations with practical judgement. Students learn not only how to train models, but also how to decide whether a model is trustworthy, appropriate for the data, and useful for real analytical questions.
